sentences.english: Welcome back to "Daily Growth," the podcast where we talk about small habits that make a big difference. Today's topic: learning a second language.
sentences.korean: '데일리 그로쓰'에 돌아오신 것을 환영합니다. 작은 습관이 큰 차이를 만드는 팟캐스트죠. 오늘 주제는 제2외국어 학습입니다.

sentences.english: Now, I know what some of you are thinking — "I tried learning Spanish in high school and I was terrible at it." But here's the thing: the way languages are taught in most schools is not the way our brains actually learn languages.
sentences.korean: 여러분 중 일부가 생각하시는 거 알아요 — '고등학교 때 스페인어 배우려 했는데 정말 못했어.'

sentences.english: The biggest mistake people make is focusing too much on grammar rules and vocabulary lists. That's like trying to learn to swim by reading a textbook about swimming. You have to get in the water.
sentences.korean: 사람들이 가장 많이 하는 실수는 문법 규칙과 단어 목록에 너무 집중하는 거예요.

sentences.english: Research in language acquisition shows that the single most important factor in learning a language is comprehensible input — that means listening to and reading content that you can mostly understand, with just a few new words mixed in.
sentences.korean: 언어 습득 연구에 따르면 언어를 배우는 데 가장 중요한 요소는 이해 가능한 입력이에요.

sentences.english: Not too easy, not too hard. Think of it as the Goldilocks zone of language learning. So my advice?
sentences.korean: 너무 쉽지도 어렵지도 않은 거죠. 언어 학습의 골디락스 존이라고 생각하세요. 제 조언이요?

sentences.english: Start by finding content you actually enjoy — podcasts, YouTube channels, TV shows — in your target language. Use subtitles at first, then gradually remove them.
sentences.korean: 실제로 즐기는 콘텐츠를 찾는 것부터 시작하세요 — 배우려는 언어로 된 팟캐스트, 유튜브, TV쇼요.

sentences.english: Don't worry about understanding every word. Your brain is doing the work even when you feel lost. Consistency matters more than intensity.
sentences.korean: 모든 단어를 이해하려고 하지 마세요. 길을 잃은 것 같을 때도 뇌는 일하고 있어요.

sentences.english: Fifteen minutes a day, every day, will beat a three-hour cramming session once a week. Every single time.
sentences.korean: 매일 15분이 주 1회 3시간 벼락치기를 항상 이겨요. 매번요.

quiz.question: What does '이해 가능한 입력 (언어 습득 이론)' mean in English?
